colorize.c(3) Library Functions Manual colorize.c(3)

NAME

colorize.c - Log message colorization for terminal output.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


const char * colorize_log_message (const char *message)
Colorize a log message for terminal output.

Detailed Description

Log message colorization for terminal output.

Definition in file colorize.c.

Function Documentation

const char * colorize_log_message (const char * message)

Colorize a log message for terminal output. Uses 4 rotating static buffers like colored_string() to handle multiple colorizations in a single expression.

Only applies colors if output is going to a TTY (not piped/redirected). Only colorizes text that is not already colored (in reset state).

Definition at line 509 of file colorize.c.

509                                                       {
510   if (!message) {
511     return message;
512   }
513 
514   // Check if colors should be used based on TTY status (same as ASCII art)
515   // Colors are only applied when output is actually a TTY
516   bool should_colorize = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
517 
518   if (!should_colorize) {
519     return message;
520   }
521 
522   // Use 4 static buffers for rotation (handles nested calls)
523   static char buffers[4][4096];
524   static int buffer_index = 0;
525 
526   char *output = buffers[buffer_index];
527   buffer_index = (buffer_index + 1) % 4;
528 
529   size_t out_pos = 0;
530   const size_t max_size = sizeof(buffers[0]);
531 
532   // Process the input string
533   for (size_t i = 0; message[i] != '\0' && out_pos < max_size - 100; i++) {
534     size_t end_pos = 0;
535 
536     // Check if already colorized - only colorize if NOT already colored
537     bool can_colorize = !ansi_is_already_colorized(message, i);
538 
539     // Try key=value pair first (highest priority pattern)
540     size_t key_end = 0, value_start = 0, value_end = 0;
541     if (can_colorize && is_key_value_pair(message, i, &key_end, &value_start, &value_end)) {
542       size_t key_len = key_end - i;
543       size_t value_len = value_end - value_start;
544 
545       // Extract key
546       char key_buf[256];
547       safe_snprintf(key_buf, sizeof(key_buf), "%.*s", (int)key_len, message + i);
548 
549       // Extract value
550       char value_buf[512];
551       safe_snprintf(value_buf, sizeof(value_buf), "%.*s", (int)value_len, message + value_start);
552 
553       // Color the key (magenta)
554       const char *colored_key = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, key_buf);
555       size_t colored_key_len = strlen(colored_key);
556 
557       // Check if we have enough space for key + equals + value
558       // (accounting for ANSI codes which will be added by colored_string)
559       if (out_pos + colored_key_len + 1 + value_len + 100 < max_size) {
560         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored_key, colored_key_len);
561         out_pos += colored_key_len;
562 
563         // Add uncolored equals sign
564         output[out_pos++] = '=';
565 
566         // Determine value color and add colored value
567         log_color_t value_color = get_value_color(value_buf);
568         const char *colored_value = colored_string(value_color, value_buf);
569         size_t colored_value_len = strlen(colored_value);
570         if (out_pos + colored_value_len < max_size) {
571           memcpy(output + out_pos, colored_value, colored_value_len);
572           out_pos += colored_value_len;
573         }
574       }
575       i = value_end - 1;
576       continue;
577     }
578 
579     // Try numeric pattern
580     if (can_colorize && is_numeric_pattern(message, i, &end_pos)) {
581       size_t pattern_len = end_pos - i;
582       char pattern_buf[256];
583       safe_snprintf(pattern_buf, sizeof(pattern_buf), "%.*s", (int)pattern_len, message + i);
584 
585       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, pattern_buf);
586       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
587       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
588         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
589         out_pos += colored_len;
590       }
591       i = end_pos - 1;
592       continue;
593     }
594 
595     // Try file path
596     if (can_colorize && is_file_path(message, i, &end_pos)) {
597       size_t path_len = end_pos - i;
598       char path_buf[512];
599       safe_snprintf(path_buf, sizeof(path_buf), "%.*s", (int)path_len, message + i);
600 
601       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, path_buf);
602       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
603       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
604         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
605         out_pos += colored_len;
606       }
607       i = end_pos - 1;
608       continue;
609     }
610 
611     // Try environment variable
612     if (can_colorize && is_env_var(message, i, &end_pos)) {
613       size_t var_len = end_pos - i;
614       char var_buf[256];
615       safe_snprintf(var_buf, sizeof(var_buf), "%.*s", (int)var_len, message + i);
616 
617       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, var_buf);
618       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
619       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
620         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
621         out_pos += colored_len;
622       }
623       i = end_pos - 1;
624       continue;
625     }
626 
627     // Try URL
628     if (can_colorize && is_url(message, i, &end_pos)) {
629       size_t url_len = end_pos - i;
630       char url_buf[2048];
631       safe_snprintf(url_buf, sizeof(url_buf), "%.*s", (int)url_len, message + i);
632 
633       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, url_buf);
634       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
635       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
636         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
637         out_pos += colored_len;
638       }
639       i = end_pos - 1;
640       continue;
641     }
642 
643     // Regular character - just copy
644     if (out_pos < max_size - 1) {
645       output[out_pos++] = message[i];
646     }
647   }
648 
649   output[out_pos] = '\0';
650   return output;
651 }




References ansi_is_already_colorized(),
    colored_string(), LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_FATAL,
    LOG_COLOR_GREY, LOG_COLOR_INFO, safe_snprintf(), and
    terminal_should_color_output().


Referenced by log_msg(), log_plain_msg(),
    log_recolor_plain_entry(), and log_template_apply().
